channel.title: Filters on Ruijun Lin 林睿军
channel.description: Recent content in Filters on Ruijun Lin 林睿军

item.title: Connections between Topological and Categorical Limits
item.description:
Filter Definition

A filter on a set $X$ is a collection $\mathcal{F}$ satisfying:

  1. downward directed,
  2. nonempty,
  3. upward closed, and
  4. proper (excludes the empty set).

Key observations:

  • Filters are closed under finite intersections.
  • The power set $2^X$ is a filter but not proper.
  • A filterbase is downward directed and nonempty; any filterbase generates a filter through upward closure.

Examples

Trivial filter: The smallest filter ${X}$ on any set $X$.

Eventuality filter: For a sequence ${x_n}$ in a space $X$:
